opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I read my test results online and it says I have stomach antibodies. My heart is racing out of my chest, I feel sick, and I'm convinced I'm bleeding internally.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Saw an abnormal result on a screening report about an hour ago, looked it up online, and now feels physically terrible and terrified something serious is happening.

symptom_narrative.onset: came on suddenly about an hour ago, right after reading the test results and researching them online
symptom_narrative.character: heart pounding and racing, feeling sick in the stomach, a tight squeezing feeling in the upper belly, and feeling like it is hard to catch a breath
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: feels really intense and frightening — convinced something is badly wrong
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: chest (pounding heart) and the upper part of the tummy
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: started all at once after reading the report and has not really let up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: reading more about it online and thinking about cancer or bleeding makes it worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: nausea and feeling short of breath / breathing fast; feels wound-up and panicky
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: does not mention any previous episode like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: too distressed to settle; has multiple browser tabs open researching stomach cancer

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: was reviewing recent routine screening blood-test results at home without a clinician to explain them; has been searching online about the result
